papers.claim: LLMs are best positioned as an interaction and explainability layer over combinatorial/MILP optimization rather than as the core optimization method.
papers.evidence: Decision identifies future-facing what-if and stakeholder support value, but not core contribution to integrated production-inventory-distribution modeling (defer).

papers.claim: A thesis can credibly position MILP as the structural backbone and add RL/heuristic modules for tractability in end-to-end integrated supply chains.
papers.evidence: Decision reports integrated production-transport-storage-shipping MILP plus PPO and relax-and-fix acceleration with consistent full-review support.

papers.claim: Constraint-learning within MIO provides a principled way to augment MILP when parts of the decision structure are data-derived.
papers.evidence: Decision cites embedding learned constraints/objectives with trust-region and ensemble mechanisms, highly aligned with MILP-first advanced extensions.

papers.claim: Detailed production-inventory MIP formulations with operational constraints provide a practical baseline block for broader integrated thesis models.
papers.evidence: Decision reports multi-product, multi-line, multi-period constraints and CPLEX case gains, while acknowledging limited distribution/supplier integration.

papers.claim: Cross-domain evidence supports positioning learning-assisted MILP as speed-enhancing with explicit guarantee tradeoffs in repeated planning contexts.
papers.evidence: Decision documents exact optimization core plus ML screening/warm-start/subspace methods and quantified speedups (4.3x with guarantees, 10.2x without).

papers.claim: Logistics-performance indicators are suitable for contextual framing and parameter realism, not as direct evidence for optimization-method contributions.
papers.evidence: Decision states strong background relevance on delays/reliability KPIs but no prescriptive MILP or advanced optimization formulation (defer).

papers.claim: Matheuristics that combine GA with MILP support a thesis position of exact-structure retention plus scalable search for integrated planning.
papers.evidence: Decision indicates direct production-distribution integration focus and sufficient low-uncertainty evidence for GA+MILP in supply-chain planning.

papers.claim: A MILP-first thesis can use ML-for-CO taxonomies to justify where learning augmentation is methodologically appropriate and where risks remain.
papers.evidence: Decision highlights decision-point and generalization-risk screening value, with high methodological but limited domain-model specificity.

papers.claim: Forecast-plus-control pipelines can be positioned as screened advanced extensions that improve inventory decisions in narrower operational scopes.
papers.evidence: Decision reports LR/RF/SVR forecasting coupled with dynamic (t,R,S) inventory control outperforming EOQ, but not full integrated network modeling.

papers.claim: ML-augmented branch-and-bound is a valid post-formulation acceleration path for MILP-centric thesis workflows.
papers.evidence: Decision cites branching/cuts/node selection/heuristics/configuration acceleration relevance, while noting lack of supply-chain-specific model evidence.

papers.claim: Iterative prediction-and-optimization can preserve MILP rigor while capturing demand-network feedback in logistics network design.
papers.evidence: Decision reports ANN+MILP iterative framework with heuristic decomposition for scaling and strong alignment to baseline-plus-extension thesis structure.

papers.claim: Strategic network-design optimization with cooperation analysis broadens thesis positioning from operational MILP to digital-manufacturing strategic coordination.
papers.evidence: Decision notes formal objective/constraint model solved via branch-and-bound plus evolutionary-game layer and case cost-reduction evidence.

papers.claim: Prescriptive ML for repeated mixed-integer convex solves supports feasibility-focused AI-OR extensions atop exact optimization baselines.
papers.evidence: Decision reports OPT-based repeated-solve advantages with strong relevance and sufficient full-review support for advanced-methods positioning.

papers.claim: Integrated location-production-inventory-distribution exact optimization is a core anchor for positioning the thesis around coordinated decision superiority over sequential planning.
papers.evidence: Decision reports direct scope match, integrated-vs-sequential benchmarking, and practical extensions (time windows, multi-echelon, environmental criteria).
